title: A multi-modal AI framework integrating Siamese networks and few-shot learning for early fetal health risk assessment
author: Yenkikar, Singh, et al.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: This section outlines the dataset, preprocessing, model structure, training process, and the metrics used in this research.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: As shown in Table 3, post-training INT8 quantization reduced the model size by ∼40 × (400 MB → <10 MB) with only a negligible accuracy loss (<0.5 %) and a modest latency increase (1.9 s → 2.1 s/report). This makes the framework highly suitable for low-resource clinical environments and portable ultrasound systems. Post-training INT8 quantization reduced the SNN model size from ∼400 MB to <10 MB, with only a negligible accuracy loss of <0.5 percentage points and a modest increase in inference latency (1.9 s → 2.1 s/report)Table 3Performance comparison of FP32 vs. INT8 quantized Siamese Neural Network (SNN).Table 3Model PrecisionSize (MB)Accuracy ( %)Inference Latency (per report)RemarksFP32 (baseline)∼400 MB98.6∼1.9 sHigh memory, GPU/CPU requiredINT8 (PTQ)<10 MB98.1∼2.1 s40 × smaller, portable for edge devices
